Warning.LootNoToken: Place {actor}'s token on the scene before dropping items.
Warning.LootNoReceiver: Select the token that should pick this up first.
Warning.LootPickupDenied: You do not have permission to pick this up.
Warning.LootDropDenied: You do not have permission to drop this item.

Ammunition.CompatibleWeaponTypesHelp: Select which weapon types can use this ammunition. If none selected, ammo is universal.

Roll.Title: Roll Test
Roll.NoTokenForActor: No token found for the attacking actor.
Roll.TargetFirst: Target a token first, then click Select Target.
Roll.NoTargetSelected: No target — target a token, then Select Target
Roll.NotEnoughAmmo: Not enough ammo to perform action. Do you need to reload?

Assistance.Label: Assistants
Assistance.Tooltip: Each assistant grants +10 to the test, up to the table cap (core.md §"Assistance", p. 25).

TryingAgain.BlockedMessage: RAW: this skill cannot be retried within the same scene without a meaningful change in circumstances.
TryingAgain.CumulativeMessage: RAW: each retry within the same scene takes a cumulative -10 to the test.

ExtendedTest.InProgress: Roll again to accumulate more DoS.

Climbing.SheerTooltip: Sheer cliffs, icy crevasses, smooth building walls, hive buttresses. Errata p. 113.

Possession.Title: Possession
Possession.UnleashUnavailable: No Unleash Daemon uses remaining this session.
Possession.ResetSessionTitle: Reset per-session Unleash Daemon counter (GM only)
Possession.ResetGmOnly: Only the GM can reset the possession session counter.
Possession.FrenzyLoopHint: Possession sustained — roll the Frenzy test each round.
Possession.FrenzyTestTitle: Roll the per-round Challenging (+0) Willpower test; failure inflicts Frenzy this round (the power must still be sustained).
Possession.MismanifestTitle: Resolve a Psychic Phenomena result: opposed Willpower test vs the Daemon; a loss escalates to full possession.
Possession.FrenzyResisted: The psyker holds — no Frenzy this round.
Possession.FrenzyEntered: The psyker's will buckles — Frenzied this round (the power must still be sustained).
Possession.MismanifestHeld: The psyker restrains the intruding Daemon and retains control.
Possession.MismanifestLost: The Daemon overwhelms the psyker — full possession.
Possession.FrenzyLoopUnavailable: The Possession power is not currently sustained.
Possession.FrenzyEnteredNotify: Frenzy test failed — the psyker is Frenzied this round.
Possession.FrenzyResistedNotify: Frenzy test passed — control held this round.
Possession.MismanifestEscalated: Mismanifest lost — escalated to full possession.

Mortification.ButtonTooltip: Self-inflict 1 Fatigue to gain +10 to all Willpower tests for 60 rounds.
Mortification.ChatBody: {name} mortifies the flesh, accepting fatigue in exchange for unshakable resolve.

Shock.SnapOutTooltip: Willpower test (Challenging +0). Success: reduce Shock by 1.

Fanatic.ButtonLabel: Death to All Who Oppose Me
Fanatic.ButtonTooltip: Spend 1 Fate point to gain +10 Weapon Skill and +10 Ballistic Skill for 5 rounds.
Fanatic.ChatTitle: Death to All Who Oppose Me
Fanatic.ChatBody: {name} screams defiance and hurls themselves at the foe — death to all who oppose them.
Fanatic.NoFatePoints: No Fate points remaining to spend.

CharacteristicSetup.Title: Characteristic Setup
CharacteristicSetup.Instructions: Enter your dice rolls in the bank, then drag them to assign to characteristics.
CharacteristicSetup.ClickToEdit: Click any roll chip to enter a value (2D20 result: 2-40)
CharacteristicSetup.AdvancedHint: Enable to customize base values for non-human characters
CharacteristicSetup.NotAllAssigned: Please assign all rolls before applying.

CharGen.RollAllHint: Roll a value for every characteristic
CharGen.PointBuyOverspent: You have spent more points than the pool allows. Reduce a characteristic before continuing.

Utility.OriginPathError: Failed to open Origin Path Builder

ConvertSystem.Title: Convert Character System
ConvertSystem.Warning: This creates a replacement actor in the target system, preserves compatible fields, repoints linked scene tokens, and removes the original actor.
ConvertSystem.NotConvertible: Only player-character actors can be converted between systems.

RighteousFury.Title: ⚡ Righteous Fury! ⚡
RighteousFury.Description: You scored a devastating hit with {weapon}!
RighteousFury.ConfirmedMessage: Additional d10 damage and critical effect!
RighteousFury.NotConfirmedMessage: The blow was powerful, but not devastating.

BeyondHomeworld.DialogDescription: Reference for the three new home worlds introduced in Enemies Beyond: Daemon World, Penal Colony, and Quarantine World.

Skills.AddFirstSpecializationHint: Use the add button above to create your first specialization

Skill.ClickNameToRoll: Use the die button to roll

Favourites.StarSkillsHint: Star skills in the Statistics tab to add them here
Favourites.StarTalentsHint: Star talents in the Talents tab to add them here

AdditionalUse.DescriptionPlaceholder: Describe this use of the skill…

WH40K.FateActionExpired: Action data expired. Unable to spend Fate Point.
WH40K.FateAlreadySpent: A Fate Point has already been spent on this roll. Enable 'Multiple Fate Burn Per Roll' in system settings to allow stacking.
WH40K.FateInsufficient: Actor does not have enough Fate Points!
WH40K.FateAddDoSNotSuccess: Cannot add a Degree of Success to a failed roll.
WH40K.FateRerollConfirmContent: Spend a Fate Point to re-roll this test?
WH40K.FateAddDoSConfirmTitle: Spend Fate: Add Degree of Success
WH40K.FateAddDoSConfirmContent: Spend a Fate Point to add one Degree of Success to this test?
WH40K.FateAddDoSChat: {name} spent a Fate Point to add a Degree of Success (now {dos} DoS).

DamageDieReplacement.ButtonTooltip: DH2 core rules (p. 247): replace one damage die with the attack's Degrees of Success ({dos}).
DamageDieReplacement.ReplacedDieMessage: {weapon} replaced a damage die ({previous}) with {dos} DoS — new total: {total}.

AssassinsStrike.ButtonTooltip: DH2 errata (L75): after a successful melee attack, attempt a Challenging (+0) Acrobatics Test. On success, move up to a Half Move distance as a Free Action.
AssassinsStrike.SuccessChatLine: Assassin's Strike: on a successful Acrobatics test, move up to a Half Move (Agility bonus metres) as a Free Action.
AssassinsStrike.FailureChatLine: Assassin's Strike: the Acrobatics test failed — no Free Action movement is granted.

WH40K.Name: Name

Armour.DragModification: Drag an armour modification here to install it

Tooltip.Half: Click to set Half Move as active movement mode
Tooltip.Full: Click to set Full Move as active movement mode
Tooltip.Charge: Click to set Charge as active movement mode
Tooltip.Run: Click to set Run as active movement mode

WorldVersion.Name: World Version
WorldVersion.Hint: Used to handle data migration during system upgrades.

ProcessActiveEffectsDuringCombat.Name: Active Effect Processing
ProcessActiveEffectsDuringCombat.Hint: Process effects like Fire or Blood Loss on combat turn change.

SimpleAttackRolls.Name: Simple Attack Rolls
SimpleAttackRolls.Hint: Changes the default weapon automation behavior to disabled. Attack rolls will trigger a WeaponSkill or BallisticSkill roll as needed.

SimplePsychicRolls.Name: Simple Psychic Rolls
SimplePsychicRolls.Hint: Changes the default psychic power automation behavior to disabled. Psychic rolls will trigger a simple WillPower roll.

AutoPsychicPhenomena.Name: Auto-roll Psychic Phenomena
AutoPsychicPhenomena.Hint: When a psychic power triggers phenomena (per PR sufficiency and doubles rules), automatically draw from the Psychic Phenomena roll table. A draw of 75+ auto-cascades to the Perils of the Warp table. Disable to let the GM draw manually via the sheet buttons.

PrimaryGameSystem.Name: Primary Game System
PrimaryGameSystem.Hint: The 40K RPG line this world runs. Sets the default line for newly-created actors and the active line used to resolve homologated content viewed outside an actor (e.g. compendium items).

DH2Ruleset.Name: DH2e Economy Ruleset
DH2Ruleset.Hint: RAW uses only Influence + Requisition (Throne Gelt hidden). Homebrew adds Throne Gelt as street-level currency and keeps Influence at 0 until earned (no starting roll).

ResyncOnReady.Name: Resync Embedded Items From Compendiums on World Boot
ResyncOnReady.Hint: On every world load (GM only), reconcile every embedded item that originated from a compendium with its current source. Definition fields (description, mechanics, classification) are overwritten; per-actor state (skill advances, ammo counts, equipped flags, modifications, quantities) is preserved. Set flags.wh40k-rpg.frozenFromCompendium = true on a specific item to opt that one out.

ReconcileOriginGrantsOnReady.Name: Reconcile Origin-Path Grants on World Boot
ReconcileOriginGrantsOnReady.Hint: On every world load (GM only), re-apply each actor's embedded origin paths idempotently so missing trained-skill grants self-heal without re-running character creation. Characteristic/wounds/fate contributions are reconciled against a recorded per-origin delta (no double-counting); skills, talents, and the origin item itself are skip-if-exists. Safe to run every boot.

DegreesMode.Name: Degrees of Success Calculation
DegreesMode.Hint: How degrees of success/failure are counted. RAW resolves per game system. Gen 1 (DH1, Deathwatch, Rogue Trader): one extra degree per full 10 points of margin — floor(|target − roll| / 10). Gen 2 (Black Crusade, Only War, Dark Heresy 2e): 1 + the difference of the tens digits.

PromptIncompleteOriginPath.Name: Prompt to Build Unfinished Characters
PromptIncompleteOriginPath.Hint: When a character or NPC sheet is opened and its origin path has never been completed, show a prompt offering to open the origin-path builder. Players can dismiss it per-character.

FreeformCharacters.Name: Freeform Character Editing
FreeformCharacters.Hint: When enabled, content items (special abilities, malignancies, mutations, mental disorders) and world origin paths can be edited directly in Foundry, and items such as origin paths may be dragged onto an actor to build a character outside the Origin Path Builder. When disabled (default), these surfaces are read-only and the builder is the supported path. To make changes that persist across worlds and updates, edit the source in its compendium instead.

MovementAutomation.Name: Movement Automation
MovementAutomation.Hint: Controls whether token movement enforces character movement speeds via the ruler.

CharacteristicOffset.Name: Characteristic Offset
CharacteristicOffset.Hint: Integer added to the 20-point baseline used during character generation. Effective base = 20 + offset. May be negative.

PointBuyPool.Name: Point-Buy Pool
PointBuyPool.Hint: Total points a player may spend in point-buy character generation. Each +1 above a characteristic's base costs 1 point. Defaults to 100.

MultipleFateBurnPerRoll.Name: Multiple Fate Burn Per Roll (Homebrew)
MultipleFateBurnPerRoll.Hint: Allow spending more than one Fate Point on the same roll (e.g., re-roll AND add a Degree of Success). RAW (Dark Heresy 2e) permits only one Fate spend per Test.

AutoRollDamage.Name: Auto-roll Damage on Hit
AutoRollDamage.Hint: When a weapon or psychic attack succeeds, automatically roll its damage and post the damage card. Disable to require clicking the chat "Roll Damage" button manually.

AutoApplyDamage.Name: Auto-apply Damage to Target
AutoApplyDamage.Hint: When a GM resolves a damage roll made against a selected target, automatically apply the reduced damage (armour by hit location + Toughness Bonus) to the target's wounds — rolling critical damage and applying its status on overflow — instead of clicking the chat "Assign Damage" button. Player-rolled attacks still apply via the button. Defaults to off.

RequireCombatToAttack.Name: Require Active Combat to Attack
RequireCombatToAttack.Hint: When enabled, weapon attacks can only be initiated during an active combat encounter that the attacker's token is part of. Disable for out-of-combat or narrative attacks. Defaults to on.

Advancement.Title: Character Advancement
Advancement.PurchaseTalentViaAdvancement: Open the Advancement window to purchase {name} with XP — talents can't be dragged directly onto an acolyte.
Advancement.PsyRatingMaximum: Psy Rating is already at maximum (10).
Advancement.NoSkillsAvailable: No skills available at this rank
Advancement.NoTalentsAvailable: No talents available at this rank
Advancement.NoCareerMessage: You must complete your Origin Path and select a career before you can purchase advancements.
Advancement.NoCareerHint: Open the Biography tab and use the Origin Path Builder

Error.MaxedOut: This characteristic is already at maximum
Error.CannotAfford: Not enough XP to purchase this advancement

Tooltip.Overview: Overview — character summary and at-a-glance vitals
Tooltip.Status: Status — wounds, fate, fatigue, corruption, insanity & conditions
Tooltip.Combat: Combat — weapons, attacks & the combat station
Tooltip.Equipment: Equipment — weapons, armour, gear & carried items

Combat.Title: Combat
Combat.ResetActions: Reset action budget for a fresh turn
Combat.CoverAPTooltip: Cover doesn't penalise the attack roll — it adds this Armour at the hit location, absorbing part of the damage.
Combat.NoActiveCombatAttack: Attacks can only be made during an active combat encounter. Start an encounter and add this token to the tracker first.
Combat.NotYourTurnMove: It is not this combatant's turn — only the active combatant may move.
Combat.OverMovementBudget: That move exceeds the movement remaining this turn ({remaining}m left). Spend a Charge or Run action for more.
Combat.InitiativeDialogHelp: Add a situational modifier (Low/High Gravity, Constant Vigilance swap, etc) or burn a Fate Point to take 10 instead of rolling 1d10.
Combat.InitiativeFateBurn: Burn a Fate Point (take 10 instead of 1d10)

Actions.ClickHint: Click any action for a personal description. Shift+Click to post it to chat. Action types:
Actions.ButtonTitleClickForDescription: Click for description, Shift+Click to post to chat
Actions.TalentNoDescription: No description available for this talent.

Manacles.Name: Manacled
Manacles.Description: Bound in manacles — a -40 penalty applies to all Ballistic and Weapon Skill tests until the restraints are removed.
Manacles.AppliedNotification: Manacled condition applied — -40 BS/WS until removed.

Biography.ConnectionsHint: Narrative relationships. The Peer, Enemy, and Hatred talents are separate purchases and live on the Talents tab.

Daemonhost.DialogIntro: Select a Binding Strength tier for the host being summoned.

SancticPurity.PromptBody: Spend 1 Fate to negate this Phenomena roll?
SancticPurity.NegatedChatBody: The Emperor's Anathema rejects the warp's intrusion; the Phenomena roll is suppressed.

DaemonicImmunities.Tooltip: Daemonic creatures are immune to disease and poison, and possess the Undying trait — once per session, the first time they would die they instead recover to 1 wound.

SisterOfBattle.DialogTitle: Apply Sister of Battle Elite Advance
SisterOfBattle.Description: Confirm the Adepta Sororitas elite advance for the applicant. The three Sister of Battle talents below will be granted on apply.
SisterOfBattle.HolyAegisSummary: Once per round, ignore 1d10 damage from a daemonic source.

WithinHomeworld.Description: Reference card for the three homeworlds introduced by the Within supplement. GM use during character creation.

MutantBackground.Description: Confirm the Mutant origin path background. The applicant begins play with starting Corruption and the Twisted Flesh talent, allowing failed Malignancy tests to be converted into Mutations.
MutantBackground.TwistedFleshNote: When you fail a Malignancy test, you may convert the malignancy into a Mutation instead. The GM resolves the conversion from the Mutation table.
MutantBackground.AppliedMessage: The Mutant background has been applied to

AerialManoeuvre.FreeAttack: Pilot-controlled weapons may fire at the target immediately as a Free Action.

LockOn.Name: Lock On
LockOn.OutcomeSuccess: The pilot manoeuvres behind the foe. The pilot and crew gain +20 BS against that craft.
LockOn.OutcomeFail: The pilot fails to gain position. Other enemies gain +10 BS against the pilot's craft until the start of the pilot's next turn.

TightTurn.Name: Tight Turn
TightTurn.OutcomeSuccess: The flyer wrenches around — 90° turns this move, and Altitude may shift one level.
TightTurn.OutcomeFail: The turn is mishandled; the flyer drops one Altitude tier.
TightTurn.OutcomeDestabilise: The turn is mishandled at ground level; the flyer destabilises (resolve on the Out-of-Control table).

CriticalDamage.NoEffectText: Critical Effect text unavailable (content pack not installed).

TwoWeaponWielder.RefocusSummary: Errata p. 132: after a Half-Action attack with one weapon, make a single additional same-mode Half-Action attack with the other weapon as a Free Action.
TwoWeaponWielder.FreeActionNote: The off-hand attack follows the same restrictions as the opener and costs no further Action.

MedicaeMechadendrite.DialogIntro: Engage the flesh staplers to staunch Blood Loss as a Half Action.
MedicaeMechadendrite.HalfActionNote: Staunching Blood Loss with the flesh staplers costs a Half Action (errata p. 183).
MedicaeMechadendrite.NotEligible: This actor has no Medicae Mechadendrite cybernetic, or this game system does not use it.
MedicaeMechadendrite.BloodLossStaunched: The flesh staplers seal the wound. Blood Loss is staunched.
MedicaeMechadendrite.StaunchFailed: The staplers fail to close the wound. Blood Loss persists.

SancticDaemonology.SoulBindingAvailable: Soul Binding may ignore this Phenomena (once per session).
SancticDaemonology.AnathemaAvailable: Emperor's Anathema may spend 1 Fate to negate this Phenomena.

Cybernetics.ConsequenceBloodLoss: Blood Loss + 1d5 damage at the install site
Cybernetics.ConsequenceFaulty: Implant fitted faulty — functions one craftsmanship tier lower until re-installed

Crusader.ButtonTooltip: Spend 1 Fate point to automatically pass a Fear test with Degrees of Success equal to your Willpower Bonus.
Crusader.ChatBody: {name} steels their soul and calls upon the Emperor's grace — the Fear test passes by sheer faith.
Crusader.NoFatePoints: No Fate points remaining to spend.

WithoutHomeworld.DialogDescription: Reference for the three new home worlds introduced in Enemies Without: Death World, Garden World, and Research Station.

PushTheLimit.MotiveSystemsTable: Table 7-32: Motive Systems Critical Hit Effects (DH Core p. 257)
PushTheLimit.ImpactLegTable: Table 7-18: Impact Critical Effects — Leg (DH Core p. 239)

FailEffect.hit: Target is hit by the attack.
FailEffect.burning: Target catches fire and gains the Burning condition.
FailEffect.snared: Target is Snared (immobilised) until they break free.
FailEffect.haywire: Technological items roll on the Haywire table.
FailEffect.poisoned: Target suffers 1d10 additional damage from the toxin.
FailEffect.armour-melt: Armour points are reduced by 1d10; overflow bypasses Toughness.

PowerField.Destroyed: The parried weapon is destroyed by the power field!
PowerField.Name: Power Field
PowerField.Description: A successful parry against a non-Power, non-Force weapon destroys the parried weapon.

Maximal.Name: Maximal
Maximal.Description: Once per encounter, fire at +1d10 damage and +2 penetration; the weapon gains Overheats this shot and must Recharge afterwards.

Blast.Name: Blast (X)
Blast.Description: Hits all targets within X metres of the impact point.

Concussive.Name: Concussive (X)
Concussive.Description: On a hit, the target makes a Toughness test (-X×10) or is Stunned for 1 round per degree of failure; if damage exceeds the target's Strength Bonus, they are knocked Prone.

Corrosive.Name: Corrosive
Corrosive.Description: Rolls 1d10 against the struck location's armour; armour loses that many points and any overflow damage bypasses Toughness.

Crippling.Name: Crippling (X)
Crippling.Description: If wounded, target gains the Crippled condition; taking more than a Half Action inflicts X damage ignoring Armour and Toughness.

Flame.Name: Flame
Flame.Description: Target makes an Agility test or catches fire.

Graviton.Name: Graviton
Graviton.Description: On a hit, the target makes a Strength test or falls Prone (vehicles roll Agility instead). The attack deals additional damage equal to the struck location's Armour Points.

Hallucinogenic.Name: Hallucinogenic (X)
Hallucinogenic.Description: Target makes a Toughness test (-X×10) or rolls on the Hallucinogenic table.

Haywire.Name: Haywire (X)
Haywire.Description: On a hit, technological items within X×10 metres roll on the Haywire table at strength 1d10.

Indirect.Name: Indirect (X)
Indirect.Description: Can be fired without line of sight; the firer suffers a -10×X BS penalty and the shot scatters 1d10-BSB metres on a miss.

Lance.Name: Lance
Lance.Description: Penetration is multiplied by degrees of success (minimum 1).

Primitive.Name: Primitive (X)
Primitive.Description: Each damage die counts as X if it would otherwise roll higher (against non-Primitive armour).

Scatter.Name: Scatter
Scatter.Description: Range-banded damage: +3 at Point Blank, +0 at Short Range, -3 at Standard / Long / Extreme Range.

Shocking.Name: Shocking
Shocking.Description: On a hit, the target makes a Toughness test or suffers 1 level of Fatigue and is Stunned for a number of rounds equal to half the degrees of failure (round up).

Smoke.Name: Smoke (X)
Smoke.Description: On detonation, creates a smoke cloud X metres across that grants concealment.

Snare.Name: Snare (X)
Snare.Description: On a hit, the target makes an Agility test (-X×10) or is Snared until they escape with a Strength or Agility test (-X×10) as a Full Action.

Spray.Name: Spray
Spray.Description: No BS test required; all targets in a cone make a Challenging (+0) Agility test to avoid being hit. Leaping Dodge upgrades the test to Dodge.

Toxic.Name: Toxic (X)
Toxic.Description: On a wound, target makes a Toughness test (-X×10) or suffers 1d10 additional damage of the weapon's damage type.

RightStuff.DialogIntro: Spend a Fate Point to automatically succeed at this Operate or Survival test with degrees of success equal to your Agility bonus.
RightStuff.AutoSuccessNote: The test auto-succeeds — no dice are rolled. DoS equals your Agility bonus.
RightStuff.AutoSuccessWithDoS: The test succeeds with degrees of success equal to the Ace's Agility bonus.
RightStuff.AutoSuccessZeroDoS: The test succeeds with 0 degrees of success (Agility bonus is 0).
RightStuff.FateSpentTag: Spends a Fate Point — Right Stuff
RightStuff.NotAce: Only an Ace may invoke Right Stuff (without.md p. 39).
RightStuff.NoFate: No Fate points remaining to spend.

Advancement.RecheckTitle: Re-evaluate Alignment at this Corruption-Point threshold
Advancement.PendingFlip: A re-check would flip this Heretic's Alignment to {target}.
Advancement.PsykerLocked: Aligned to Khorne — psychic powers and Psyker Trait are suppressed.
Advancement.InfamyCapped: Cap reached — Infamy advances must be earned through play.
Advancement.BuyInfamyConfirmBody: Spend {xp} xp to raise Infamy by {step} (to {next})?
Advancement.BuyInfamyInsufficientXP: Not enough XP to purchase Infamy advance (cost: {cost}, available: {available}).
Advancement.BuyInfamyCapped: Infamy is already at the {cap} cap and cannot be advanced with xp.

Contempt.Success: The Warp yields — the ritual takes hold.
Contempt.Failure: The Warp roils against the ritualist — the working fails.

BreakingMastery.Held: The binding holds against the assault.

Confirm.Title: Confirm Apotheosis
Confirm.Body: Commit to apotheosis? This change is permanent.

Insufficient.Infamy: Ascension blocked — Infamy below the apotheosis threshold (100).
Insufficient.Corruption: Ascension blocked — Corruption below the apotheosis threshold (70).

QuickAndTheDead.ToggleTitle: Toggle the Quick and the Dead trait

Renown.Label: Renown
Renown.GateFailure: Renown rank insufficient to requisition this item.

Earn.RankTooLow: Renown rank too low to earn this Distinction.

Advanced.Label: Advanced Special Ability
Advanced.RankTooLow: Renown rank too low to use this Advanced Special Ability.
Advanced.InsufficientCohesion: Insufficient Cohesion to activate this Advanced Special Ability.

Rally.Title: Rally — Free Action
Rally.Description: Challenging (+0) Command (or Fellowship) test to negate this turn's Cohesion loss.

Validation.RankTooLow: Renown rank too low to requisition this item.
Validation.OverAllocated: A contributing brother allocated more RP than available.
Validation.InsufficientPool: Pooled contributions do not cover the item cost.
Validation.InvalidInput: Invalid requisition input — item name and a non-negative base cost are required.

Ability.InsufficientCohesion: Insufficient Cohesion to activate this ability.

Oath.Label: Mission Oath
Oath.Sworn: Oath sworn — bound for the mission.

Validation.NotLeader: Only the kill-team leader may swear a Mission Oath.
Validation.AlreadySworn: An Oath is already sworn for this mission.

Vehicle.Label: Vehicle
Vehicle.RollCritTooltip: Roll 1d10 + over-Integrity on the Vehicle Critical Hit Chart (Table 4-2).
Vehicle.RepairTestTooltip: Tech-Use Test; difficulty scales with the last suffered critical result.

Acquisition.Title: Kill-Team Vehicle Requisition
Acquisition.InsufficientRp: Kill-team Requisition Points insufficient to acquire this vehicle.
Acquisition.RankTooLow: Renown rank too low to requisition this vehicle.

Colony.EcclesiasticalOrderSwapLabel: Ecclesiastical: take Order loss as Piety

IncorruptibleDevotion.Description: Your unwavering faith arms you against the Ruinous Powers. You may refuse this taint, taking the spiritual toll on your mind instead.
IncorruptibleDevotion.TradeMessage: Faith holds against the warp. Corruption is averted; the cost is paid in sanity.
IncorruptibleDevotion.DeclinedMessage: The trade is refused. Corruption takes hold.

Talent.MustBeOnActor: This talent must be on an actor to roll.

OriginPath.UseBuilderOnDrop: Origin paths are applied through the Origin Path Builder, not by dragging them onto a character. Open the builder from the d20 on the portrait. (A GM can enable freeform building in system settings.)
OriginPath.WorldSourcedWarning: Authored in this world, not in a compendium
OriginPath.HomebrewContent: Homebrew content — no official source in any line
OriginPath.PersistenceFootnote: Tip: edits here only affect this world item. For changes that persist across worlds and updates, edit the source in its compendium instead.

IncompletePrompt.Title: Origin Path Incomplete
IncompletePrompt.Message: Character has not completed an origin path and therefore has not been fully built. Do so now? You can always do so later by clicking the d20 on the character portrait.
IncompletePrompt.DontShowAgain: Don't show this again for this character

OriginPath.Description: Drag origin path items from the compendium to define your character's background. Each step grants unique abilities and bonuses.
OriginPath.DragHere: Drag an origin here or click Browse
OriginPath.RandomizeHint: Fill empty slots with random choices
OriginPath.RandomizeConfirm: Randomize all origin path selections? This will replace any current selections.
OriginPath.NoBonusesYet: Select origin path steps to see bonuses
OriginPath.InvalidStep: This item cannot be placed in this slot
OriginPath.ConfirmCommit: Apply this origin path to your character? This will replace your current path and apply all bonuses.
OriginPath.Name: Name
OriginPath.NoEffects: No origin path items selected. Drag items from the Origin Path compendium to build your character's background.
OriginPath.GuidedDesc: Follow the chart step by step
OriginPath.SwitchToFree: Switch to Free Selection mode - ignore path requirements
OriginPath.SwitchToGuided: Switch to Guided mode - follow path requirements
OriginPath.RollErrorGeneric: Error rolling dice. Check console for details.
OriginPath.RollPromptFirst: Please roll or input a value first!
OriginPath.SelectOriginPrompt: Select an origin from the list to see its details
OriginPath.NoOriginsAvailable: No origins available for this step
OriginPath.OriginNotAvailable: This origin is not available in guided mode
OriginPath.CompleteAllChoices: Complete all required choices before committing
OriginPath.CharacteristicsRequiredBeforeCommit: Roll or assign your characteristics before completing character creation.
OriginPath.StepInProgressEquipment: The Equip Acolyte step is still in progress — finish picking starting gear before applying the origin path.
OriginPath.StepInProgressCharacteristics: The Characteristic Roll step is still in progress — roll or assign your characteristics before applying the origin path.
OriginPath.DuplicateAptitude: Your origin path grants these aptitudes more than once: {aptitudes}. Duplicate aptitudes have no effect — under Dark Heresy 2e you may swap a duplicate for a characteristic aptitude (GM adjudication).

AptitudeDouble.BannerBody: One or more of your origin steps grants an aptitude your character already has. Per Dark Heresy 2e, pick a replacement aptitude you do not currently possess.
AptitudeDouble.PromptBody: Your origin grants the {aptitude} aptitude, but your character already has it. Pick a replacement aptitude.
AptitudeDouble.NoCandidates: No replacement aptitudes are available. Ask the GM to adjudicate.
AptitudeDouble.ResolvedBody: You resolved a duplicate aptitude by swapping it for the listed replacement. You can change your choice if you wish.

OriginPath.OpenAdvancementPrompt: Your Acolyte has {xp} XP to spend on starting advances. Open the Advancement dialog now?
OriginPath.HomeWorldDesc: Where you were born and raised shapes your fundamental nature
OriginPath.BackgroundDesc: The organisation or circumstances that trained you before the Inquisition
OriginPath.RoleDesc: Your specialised function within the warband
OriginPath.EliteDesc: An optional elite advance that further defines your character
OriginPath.CharacteristicsDesc: Roll or enter your characteristic values
OriginPath.EquipmentDesc: Select starting gear from the Armoury up to your Influence bonus. Each pick must be Scarce (-10) or better.
OriginPath.EquipmentRuleHint: Per DH2e core pg 80: select up to your starting Influence bonus in items of Scarce (-10) or better Availability. All weapons come with two clips of standard ammunition.
OriginPath.EquipmentNoneAvailable: No items match the current filters.
OriginPath.EquipmentNoInfluence: Roll or enter starting Influence before selecting equipment.
OriginPath.EquipmentNeedsCharacteristics: Assign characteristics before equipping your Acolyte.
OriginPath.HomebrewInfluenceNoRoll: Homebrew ruleset: Influence is not rolled at creation — it starts at 0 and is earned and spent during play.
OriginPath.ResetBaseStatsNote: Base characteristics are always overridden with the rolled generation values.
OriginPath.BirthrightDesc: Your social position at birth determined your early opportunities
OriginPath.LureDesc: What called you to the stars and a life among them
OriginPath.TrialsDesc: A defining event that shaped who you became
OriginPath.MotivationDesc: The core drive that pushes you forward
OriginPath.DirectionChangeWarning: Changing direction will reset all current selections. Continue?
OriginPath.ChangeSelectionWarning: Changing this selection will reset the following steps: {steps}. Do you want to continue?
OriginPath.LineageDesc: Noble bloodlines may grant additional abilities and connections. This step is optional.
OriginPath.DivinationTableUnavailable: The Divination table is unavailable — rolled {roll} on 1d100. Record the corresponding maxim from the rulebook by hand.
OriginPath.ConfirmSelectionHint: Lock in this choice and advance to the next step

Biography.ContactsHint: Friendly NPCs with a positive disposition modifier. Distinct from the Peer talent — purchase that separately for the mechanical bonus.
Biography.AdversariesHint: Hostile NPCs with a negative disposition modifier. Distinct from the Enemy / Hatred talents — purchase those separately.

Endeavours.RewardNarrativePlaceholder: Notable beats, contacts, items or favours granted on completion...
Endeavours.DescriptionPlaceholder: Notes on the Endeavour, its origin, and what the Dynasty stands to gain or lose...

ExtendedAction.Description: Per the Rogue Trader Stars of Inequity and Battlefleet Koronus rules, the bridge crew may dispatch one Extended Action per turn. Click an action below to post its rules card to chat.
ExtendedAction.Empty: No Extended Actions found in the active compendiums. Compendium content may not yet be loaded.
ExtendedAction.DispatchTitle: Post this Extended Action to chat

ManoeuvreAction.Description: Manoeuvre Actions are the bridge pilot's turn-by-turn ship handling options. Click an action below to post its rules card to chat.
ManoeuvreAction.Empty: No Manoeuvre Actions found in the active compendiums. Compendium content may not yet be loaded.
ManoeuvreAction.DispatchTitle: Post this Manoeuvre Action to chat

Combat.AllShieldsUp: All void shields are already raised.
Combat.AllShieldsDown: No void shields are currently raised.
Combat.NoShieldsExhausted: No exhausted shields remain to bring back online.

Crew.OfficersDesc: Assign characters to ship roles for bonuses.
Crew.NotifyCancelled: Prior-turn damage cancelled: +{hull} hull, +{crew} crew, +{morale} morale.

Manoeuvre.RollTitle: Roll this Manoeuvre Action (Pilot (Space Craft) + Manoeuvrability)
Manoeuvre.Empty: No Manoeuvre Actions available for this game system.

AdjustSpeed.Label: Adjust Speed
AdjustSpeed.Benefit: Move faster or slower than normal.

AdjustSpeedAndBearing.Label: Adjust Speed & Bearing
AdjustSpeedAndBearing.Benefit: Turn earlier than normal while moving faster or slower than normal.

ComeToNewHeading.Label: Come to New Heading
ComeToNewHeading.Benefit: Make two turns in one Round (-20 BS to fire this turn).

Disengage.Label: Disengage
Disengage.Benefit: Escape combat (opposed by enemy Detection+Scrutiny within 20 VU).

EvasiveManoeuvres.Label: Evasive Manoeuvres
EvasiveManoeuvres.Benefit: Inflicts -10 (per DoS) penalty on incoming fire; same penalty applies to own shooting.

Ramming.BonusDamage: Imposing / Good an' Ard prow bonus
Ramming.MissExplain: The defender out-flew the ram; no collision occurs and neither hull takes damage.

Boarding.RepelledExplain: The defenders held the breach; the boarders withdrew without inflicting damage.
Boarding.BoardersLostExplain: The defenders won the Command Test by 3+ degrees of success — the boarding party is captured or killed.

HitAndRun.ShotDownExplain: The boarding craft is destroyed. The pilot is stranded in a crippled craft or killed at the GM's discretion.
HitAndRun.NoResultExplain: The Hit-and-Run resolves with no damage to the target.

Build.OverBudget: Build exceeds the hull's Ship Points budget.
Build.NotifyCannotCommit: Cannot save: starship build is invalid.
Build.SummaryEmpty: No components, upgrades, or roles are contributing modifiers.

Critical.RollTooltip: Roll 1d5 on the Critical Hit chart (Battlefleet Koronus)
Critical.PersistsUntilRepaired: Persists until cleared by Emergency Repair or Quick Repair.
Critical.TableUnavailable: Critical Hit roll table unavailable — rolled {rolled} on 1d5. Apply the corresponding effect from Battlefleet Koronus by hand.

WarpTravel.DialogIntro: Resolve the 5-stage Navigation workflow. GM enters base voyage duration plus per-stage d100 rolls.
WarpTravel.PerilTableGap: (No table entry — re-roll or GM adjudication.)
WarpTravel.BeaconLostNote: The Astronomican signal was lost (3+ DoF on Stage 2). Stage 4 used the Hellish (-60) substitute Navigation test.
WarpTravel.OffCourseNote: Vessel translated into the wrong system / region of space (failed Stage 4 with a 9 on either d10).

Notification.CriticalFailure: Critical failure! Profit Factor reduced to {pf}

Item.PersistenceFootnote: Tip: edits here only affect this world item. For changes that persist across worlds and updates, edit the source in its compendium instead.

NPC.Name: Name
NPC.QuickCreateSubtitle: Generate a balanced NPC based on threat level and role
NPC.HordeModeHint: Creates an NPC with magnitude tracking instead of individual wounds
NPC.SignificantChangeWarning: Large threat change may significantly alter NPC balance

Horde.Title: Horde
Horde.ConvertWarning: This will convert the horde to a single NPC. Continue?

Validation.InvalidThreatLevel: Threat level must be between 1 and 30
Validation.InvalidCharacteristic: Characteristic values must be between 1 and 100

Tooltips.ThreatLevel: Higher threat = more dangerous. Affects recommended party size.
Tooltips.Magnitude: Horde health pool. Decreases as damage is taken.
Tooltips.DuplicateNPC: Create a copy of this NPC
Tooltips.CustomStat: Override calculated value with custom value

Template.Name: Template Name
Template.NoTemplatesFound: No templates found matching your filters
Template.CreateError: Failed to create NPC from template

Errors.NoCharacterForRoll: Vehicle items are rolled using the current user's character. However, no character found.
Errors.NoActionForItemType: No actions implemented for item type: {type}

Chat.NoItemFound: No item found on this chat card.
Chat.ItemNotFound: The item could not be found. It may have been deleted.
Chat.NoTokensTargeted: No tokens are currently targeted. Target one or more tokens to apply damage.

ArmourProperty.AutoStabilizedDesc: Built-in stabilization system for ranged weapons
ArmourProperty.HexagrammicDesc: Psychic protection runes provide +20 to resist psychic effects
ArmourProperty.BlessedDesc: Blessed by the Ecclesiarchy, provides bonus vs corruption
ArmourProperty.AgilityBonusDesc: Enhanced mobility systems provide Agility bonus

Modification.RestrictionsHelp: Select which armour types this modification can be applied to.

Gear.EffectHint: Describe what this item does when used (rules text, mechanical effects, etc.)
Gear.DurationHint: How long the effect lasts (for drugs, consumables, etc.)
Gear.UsesExhausted: This item has no uses remaining

NpcInteractions.Empty: No player characters in the world yet.
NpcInteractions.CapTooltip: Per DH2 errata p.125, a PC can shift this NPC's disposition through social interaction only a number of times equal to their Fellowship bonus. Above the cap, further interactions stop yielding disposition gain.

Source.AcquiredPlaceholder: Where the party got this — e.g. recovered from The Cache, Session 4

WeaponQuality.AccurateDesc: This weapon is easier to fire accurately. Aim action grants +10 bonus to hit.
WeaponQuality.InaccurateDesc: This weapon is difficult to aim. User suffers -10 penalty on attacks.
WeaponQuality.ReliableDesc: This weapon is very well made and rarely jams. Only jams on a roll of 95 or higher.
WeaponQuality.UnreliableDesc: This weapon is prone to jamming. Jams on a roll of 96 or higher.
WeaponQuality.Unreliable2Desc: This weapon is extremely prone to jamming. Jams on a roll of 90 or higher.
WeaponQuality.NeverJamDesc: This weapon is of such high quality that it never jams or overheats.
WeaponQuality.BalancedDesc: This weapon is well-balanced. User gains +10 to Parry tests with this weapon.
WeaponQuality.DefensiveDesc: When wielding this weapon, user gains +15 to Parry tests.
WeaponQuality.FastDesc: Attacks with this weapon count as Half Actions instead of Full Actions.
WeaponQuality.FlexibleDesc: This weapon can be used to entangle. User gains +20 to Grapple tests.
WeaponQuality.UnbalancedDesc: This weapon is difficult to control. User suffers -10 to Parry tests.
WeaponQuality.UnwieldyDesc: This weapon requires two hands and a Full Action to attack.
WeaponQuality.TearingDesc: This weapon rips and tears through flesh. Re-roll damage dice that show 1 or 2.
WeaponQuality.RazorSharpDesc: This weapon is incredibly sharp. Ignores 1 point of Armour.
WeaponQuality.ProvenDesc: Any damage dice that roll less than X count as X instead.
WeaponQuality.FellingDesc: Reduces target's Unnatural Toughness by X for damage calculation.
WeaponQuality.CripplingDesc: If target takes damage, they suffer -X penalty to all tests until healed.
WeaponQuality.DevastatingDesc: Against hordes, reduces Magnitude by an additional X on successful hit.
WeaponQuality.BlastDesc: Weapon hits in X metre radius. All in area must dodge or take damage.
WeaponQuality.ScatterDesc: Hits additional targets within 2 metres of primary target.
WeaponQuality.SprayDesc: Can attack multiple targets in a cone. Makes separate attack rolls.
WeaponQuality.StormDesc: Can reroll missed attacks when firing on Full Auto.
WeaponQuality.ConcussiveDesc: Target must make Toughness test at -10×X or be stunned for 1 round.
WeaponQuality.CorrosiveDesc: Permanently reduces target's Armour by 1d10 on hit.
WeaponQuality.ToxicDesc: Target must make Toughness test at -10×X or take additional 1d10 damage.
WeaponQuality.HallucinogenicDesc: Target must make Willpower test at -10×X or suffer hallucinations.
WeaponQuality.SnareDesc: Target must make Agility test at -10×X or be immobilized.
WeaponQuality.ShockingDesc: Target must make Toughness test or be stunned for 1 round.
WeaponQuality.ShockDesc: Electrical damage that can arc to nearby targets in water.
WeaponQuality.BoltDesc: Fires mass-reactive explosive rounds. Effective against all targets.
WeaponQuality.ChainDesc: Chainblade weapon that tears through flesh and armour.
WeaponQuality.FlameDesc: Ignites flammable materials. Can cause targets to catch fire.
WeaponQuality.ForceDesc: Psychically-attuned weapon. Can be activated by psykers for bonus damage.
WeaponQuality.LasDesc: Laser weapon with high accuracy and armor penetration.
WeaponQuality.MeltaDesc: At half range or less, rolls extra d10 for penetration and damage.
WeaponQuality.PlasmaDesc: Superheated plasma weapon. Can be overcharged for increased damage.
WeaponQuality.PowerDesc: Power field weapon that disrupts molecular bonds.
WeaponQuality.PowerFieldDesc: Surrounded by a disruption field that penetrates armour.
WeaponQuality.PrimitiveDesc: Against armour higher than X, weapon only inflicts 1d10-3 damage.
WeaponQuality.GrenadeDesc: Thrown weapon. Can bounce to reach targets around corners.
WeaponQuality.LauncherDesc: Fires grenades or missiles at long range.
WeaponQuality.IndirectDesc: Can fire at targets not in line of sight with spotter. Requires X actions.
WeaponQuality.HaywireDesc: Disrupts machines and electronics. Causes X hits to constructs.
WeaponQuality.OverheatsDesc: On roll of 91+, weapon overheats and cannot fire for 1 round.
WeaponQuality.OverchargeDesc: Can increase damage by X but weapon gains Overheats quality.
WeaponQuality.RechargeDesc: Weapon requires Full Action to recharge between shots.
WeaponQuality.MaximalDesc: Can overcharge for +2d10 damage but weapon gains Overheats.
WeaponQuality.SanctifiedDesc: Blessed weapon. +2 damage against daemons and warp entities.
WeaponQuality.TaintedDesc: Corrupted weapon. May cause Corruption points on use.
WeaponQuality.DaemonWepDesc: Weapon bound with a daemon. Extremely dangerous to wield.
WeaponQuality.DaemonbaneDesc: Gains Vengeful (8) quality when used against daemons.
WeaponQuality.WarpWeaponDesc: Ignores physical armour. Attacks target's Willpower.
WeaponQuality.WitchEdgeDesc: Psychically resonant blade. Psykers can channel powers through it.
WeaponQuality.RuneWepDesc: Engraved with protective runes. +10 to resist psychic powers.
WeaponQuality.GaussDesc: Necron technology. Strips away matter at molecular level. Righteous Fury triggers on 9 or 10.
WeaponQuality.GravitonDesc: Manipulates gravity. Concussive and Crippling against most targets.
WeaponQuality.NecronWepDesc: Alien technology. Ignores most forms of protection.
WeaponQuality.SmokeDesc: Creates smoke cloud of X metre radius. Blocks line of sight.
WeaponQuality.LivingAmmunitionDesc: Fires living organisms. May have unpredictable effects.
WeaponQuality.TwinLinkedDesc: Two weapons firing in unison. Can reroll failed attack.
WeaponQuality.GyroStabilisedDesc: Stabilization system. Reduces penalties for moving and shooting.
WeaponQuality.VengefulDesc: Righteous Fury triggers on rolls of X or higher on damage dice.
WeaponQuality.LanceDesc: Designed for mounted combat. +1d10 damage when charging.
WeaponQuality.DecayDesc: Living targets that take Critical Damage must make Toughness test at -10×X or suffer disease.
WeaponQuality.IrradiatedDesc: Target hit must make Toughness test at -10×X or suffer radiation poisoning.
WeaponQuality.ReactiveDesc: Can parry ranged attacks as if they were melee attacks.
WeaponQuality.UnstableDesc: May explode catastrophically if weapon jams.
WeaponQuality.VolatileDesc: Extremely dangerous if mishandled. Plasma weapons with this quality risk catastrophic failure.
WeaponQuality.IntegratedWeaponDesc: Built into armour or body. Cannot be disarmed.
WeaponQuality.OgrynProofDesc: Specially reinforced for use by Ogryns and large creatures.
WeaponQuality.SMWepDesc: Designed for Space Marines. Difficult for normal humans to use.
WeaponQuality.CustomisedDesc: Modified for specific purpose. Effects vary.
WeaponQuality.SPDesc: Special property. See weapon description for details.
WeaponQuality.CleansingFireDesc: Purifying flames that deal additional damage to unholy targets.

Reload.Title: Reload Weapon
Reload.OutOfTurnWarning: It is not your turn. Reload anyway? This will not track action economy.

Career.RogueTraderDesc: A scion of a powerful dynasty, holder of a sacred Warrant of Trade.
Career.ArchMilitantDesc: A warrior born, trained in the arts of war and destruction.
Career.AstropathDesc: A soul-bound psyker who transmits messages across the void.
Career.ExploratorDesc: A Tech-Priest of Mars, seeker of lost knowledge.
Career.MissionaryDesc: A zealous servant of the Imperial Creed.
Career.NavigatorDesc: A mutant noble who guides ships through the Warp.
Career.SeneschalDesc: A master of trade, diplomacy, and information.
Career.VoidMasterDesc: A seasoned spacefarer and ship commander.

Combat.Action.StandardAttackDesc: Make one melee or ranged attack. +10 to WS/BS.
Combat.Action.ThrowDesc: Half Action. Throw a grenade or thrown weapon (Ballistic Skill test). Range comes from the weapon; a miss scatters in a random direction.
Combat.Action.CalledShotDesc: Attack a specific location on your target with a –20 to WS or BS.
Combat.Action.AllOutAttackDesc: +20 to WS, cannot Dodge or Parry.
Combat.Action.SemiAutoBurstDesc: +0 to BS, additional hit for every two degrees of success.
Combat.Action.FullAutoBurstDesc: -10 to BS, additional hit for every degree of success.
Combat.Action.SuppressingFireDesc: Force opponents to take cover, –20 to BS.
Combat.Action.OverwatchDesc: Shoot targets coming into a set kill zone, –20 to BS.
Combat.Action.MultipleAttacksDesc: Attack more than once in the same round—requires two weapons or a talent.
Combat.Action.SwiftAttackDesc: Half Action, melee. Make multiple attacks against one target — +0 WS, one extra hit per two DoS. Requires the Swift Attack talent.
Combat.Action.LightningAttackDesc: Half Action, melee. A flurry of strikes — –10 WS, one extra hit per DoS. Requires the Lightning Attack talent.
Combat.Action.FocusPowerDesc: Manifest a known psychic power; action type varies by power. Requires Psy Rating.
Combat.Action.EvasionDesc: Reaction. Avoid an incoming attack using Dodge (ranged or melee) or Parry (melee, weapon required).
Combat.Action.MoveDesc: Move up to your movement as a Half Action or twice your movement as a Full Action.
Combat.Action.RunDesc: Move triple, enemies –20 BS and +20 WS.
Combat.Action.DisengageDesc: Break off from melee and move.
Combat.Action.StandMountDesc: Stand up or mount a riding animal.
Combat.Action.DodgeDesc: Test Dodge to negate a hit.
Combat.Action.ParryDesc: Test Weapon Skill to negate a hit.
Combat.Action.AimDesc: +10 bonus to hit as a Half Action or +20 to hit as a Full Action on your next attack.
Combat.Action.DelayDesc: Before your next Turn take any Half Action.
Combat.Action.FeintDesc: Opposed WS Test, if you win, your next attack cannot be Dodged or Parried.
Combat.Action.GrappleDesc: Affect a Grappled opponent or escape from a Grapple.
Combat.Action.DefensiveStanceDesc: Gain an additional Reaction, opponents suffer –20 to WS.
Combat.Action.BraceHeavyDesc: Prepare to fire a heavy weapon.
Combat.Action.KnockDownDesc: Try and knock an opponent to the ground.
Combat.Action.ManoeuvreDesc: Opposed WS Test, if you win, move enemy 1 metre.

CombatPanel.Title: Combat
CombatPanel.MultipleWeapons: Multiple weapons available - use character sheet to select

Corruption.Label: Corruption
Corruption.Warn90: PROFANE - On the Edge of Damnation (-30)
Corruption.DropFull: Drop Malignancies Here or Click to Create
Corruption.InfoIntro: Malignancies are physical and mental manifestations of Chaos corruption.

Insanity.Label: Insanity
Insanity.Warn100: TERMINALLY INSANE - Mind Lost to Madness!
Insanity.Warn80: DERANGED - On the Edge of Madness (-20)
Insanity.DropFull: Drop Mental Disorders Here or Click to Create
Insanity.InfoIntro: Mental disorders represent psychological trauma from witnessing horrors beyond mortal comprehension.

Fatigue.Label: Fatigue
Fatigue.Fatigued: Fatigued — Suffer –10 penalty to all Tests

VitalInfoCard.FatigueExceedsDesc: Collapse unconscious for (10 – TB) minutes. Upon waking, fatigue resets to TB.
VitalInfoCard.FatigueRecoveryDesc: Each hour of rest removes 1 fatigue. Eight consecutive hours removes all fatigue.
VitalInfoCard.FateAboutDesc: Fate Points represent the Emperor's favor and divine protection. They can be spent to alter destiny in critical moments.
VitalInfoCard.FateRerollDesc: Reroll any failed test, keeping the second result
VitalInfoCard.FateAvoidDeathDesc: When reduced to 0 wounds, spend a Fate Point to survive with 1 wound instead
VitalInfoCard.FateInterventionDesc: Call upon the Emperor's grace for a minor miracle at the GM's discretion
VitalInfoCard.FateRighteousFuryDesc: Confirm a Righteous Fury result automatically
VitalInfoCard.FateBurningDesc: Permanently spending a Fate Point (reducing your maximum) can achieve truly miraculous results, but this should only be done in the direst circumstances.
VitalInfoCard.ExperienceAboutDesc: Spend XP to improve characteristics, acquire skills and talents, or gain psychic powers. Costs vary based on the advancement being purchased.

FateUses.Intro: A Fate Point may be spent at any time to invoke the Emperor's favor. Each entry below describes one canonical use. Spending a Fate Point normally returns at the start of the next session; burning a Fate Point removes it permanently.
FateUses.Footer: Some careers, talents, and elite advances grant additional Fate Point effects. Refer to your career rules for the full list.
FateUses.RerollDesc: Re-roll any single failed Test. The second result stands, even if it is worse.
FateUses.AddBonusDesc: Add +10 to a single Test before the dice are rolled.
FateUses.CountHitTitle: Count an Attack as a Hit
FateUses.CountHitDesc: Treat a single attack as having struck, scoring one Degree of Success on the hit (additional Degrees of Success are not gained).
FateUses.RecoverWoundsDesc: Immediately recover 1d5 wounds, regardless of normal healing limits.
FateUses.RecoverStunDesc: Immediately remove all Stunned conditions and stand up if prone.
FateUses.SurviveDesc: Burn a Fate Point to survive what should have been a fatal blow. The character is left at the edge of death — battered, scarred, and permanently down one maximum Fate Point — but alive.

WeaponSheet.OnlyOneAmmoType: Only one type of ammunition can be loaded.
WeaponSheet.MustBeOnActor: This weapon must be on an actor to roll.
WeaponSheet.DoesNotUseAmmo: This weapon does not use ammunition.
WeaponSheet.ConfirmDeleteContent: Are you sure you want to remove {name} from this weapon?
WeaponSheet.DragModificationHint: Drag a weapon modification from a compendium to add it.
WeaponSheet.ModificationNotFound: Modification "{name}" not found. It may have been deleted.

InventoryGenerator.Title: Inventory Generator — {actor}
InventoryGenerator.Heading: Stock {actor}
InventoryGenerator.Hint: Draw items from the compendium for this vendor, NPC, or armoury. Profiles and weights come entirely from compendium tags.
InventoryGenerator.PoolEmpty: No compendium items are available for this game system.
InventoryGenerator.OpenTooltip: Stock this actor from the compendium
InventoryGenerator.GenerateHelp: Generate draws a weighted random selection into the staging list. Reroll redraws with fresh luck.
InventoryGenerator.BrowseTruncated: Showing the first results only — refine your search to see more.
InventoryGenerator.NothingStaged: Stage at least one item before applying.
InventoryGenerator.NothingApplied: No items could be resolved from the compendium.
InventoryGenerator.PermissionDenied: You do not have permission to modify this actor.

Loot.PickUpHint: Right-click this pile and use Pick Up, or open it to take individual items.
Loot.Title: Loot Pile

Trade.NoSourcesNearby: No trade partner is within reach.
Trade.RequestSent: Transaction request sent to the GM for approval.
Trade.NoGM: No active GM is available to approve the transaction.
Trade.OutOfRange: {source} is no longer within trading range.

Approval.Title: Approve Transaction
Approval.GMModifierHint: Negative lowers the price (a good haggle roll); positive raises it (conditions, scarcity).

Result.Rejected: Transaction request rejected by the GM.

Regiment.Label: Regiment
Regiment.NoOptions: No options available — load the OW compendium.

Validation.InsufficientAction: Not enough action available to issue this Order.
Validation.InsufficientCohesion: Not enough Cohesion to issue this Order.

Effect.RangedVolley: +5 BS to squad ranged attacks this round.
Effect.CloseQuarters: Squad gains Ganging Up bonuses against the designated foe.
Effect.TakeCover: Squad-wide cover bonus until end of round.

Chat.NoOp: No change to the Comrade's state.

Transferred.Doubles: Hit transferred to Comrade on doubles roll

Death.Insanity: Comrade dies from PC's Insanity ({amount} this round)
Death.Corruption: Comrade dies from PC's Corruption ({amount} this round)

Replacement.NotDead: Comrade is still alive — no replacement needed

Craftsmanship.Label: Craftsmanship
Craftsmanship.Empty: No equipped weapons or armour with Craftsmanship effects.

Description.EvasiveManoeuvring: Driver makes an Operate test; on success, attackers suffer a -10 penalty to hit the vehicle until the start of the driver's next turn.
Description.FloorIt: Vehicle moves at maximum tactical speed in a straight line; all ranged attacks against the vehicle suffer a -10 penalty, but the driver may take no other actions this turn.
Description.HitAndRun: Vehicle moves up to its tactical speed, gunners may fire as a free action during the move, then the vehicle continues to its end position.
Description.Jink: Reaction: driver attempts an Operate test against an incoming attack; on success the attack suffers a -20 penalty to hit.
Description.TacticalManoeuvring: Vehicle moves at tactical speed and may make a single course change of up to 90 degrees during the move.

Description.Charge: Mount and rider close to melee on a charge; the rider strikes with +10 WS, and Brutal Charge mounts add a further +20.
Description.Trample: Mount drives over an enemy in its path; resolve impact damage from the mount's profile against the target's armour and TB.
Description.RunDown: Mount pursues a fleeing target at full pace; the target must outpace the mount's Agility-based movement or be overrun.
Description.MountedAttack: Rider attacks from the saddle as a Half Action, combining situational mount-trait modifiers with the weapon-skill test.
